I am informed by the Housing Executive that NI Water connections for these planned new homes (including the 421 homes already in contract/under construction) is contingent upon new Wastewater infrastructure which had been planned for delivery under the PC21 programme. However, Apex have since been advised by NI Water that the required infrastructure will no longer progress to construction within PC21 period and there is currently no timeline for delivery of this infrastructure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Executive's decision in relation to the approval of the A5 upgrade plan.
answer_text: I am currently going through the process of Executive referral and have been providing briefing papers to Execuive colleagues. Executive agreement would enable me to make a formal announcement on the progression of the project. Until that process is complete, it is not possible to be more definitive about when I can make a formal decision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his plan to improve accessibility in the taxi industry for individuals with a disability.
answer_text: Taxi companies are directly responsible for complying with equality legislation. Under current taxi legislation there is no requirement for taxi operators to have a minimum number of wheelchair accessible vehicles, and the option to include these in their fleet is at the discretion of the operators and drivers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the zero emissions vehicle mandate.
answer_text: The Zero Emissions Vehicle Mandate has become the Vehicle Emissions Trading Schemes Order 2023 in Scotland, England, and Wales. A draft order to bring the North into the schemes was laid with the Assembly Business Office on 09 September 2024. I will be bringing a motion for debate to the Assembly in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he has considered implementing a requirement of mandatory accredited training for new care staff.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Social Care Council have developed a Level 2 Certificate in Safe and Effective Practice which is an accredited qualification. This is now available to all new voluntary, independent and statutory social care staff entering the workforce. The certificate is employment based and enables learners to gain knowledge, skills and assessed practice in the foundation skills needed to work as a social care practitioner. This certificate has also been accepted onto the NI Apprentice Framework. The Department of Health are currently developing a ten year social care workforce strategy. One of the key actions contained within this strategy is to create and implement a requirement for registered social care practitioners to complete the Level 2 Certificate after taking up employment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any discussions he has had with (i) NI Water; (ii) the Minister for Communities; and (iii) other stakeholders in relation to the impact water infrastructure issues is having on housing development.
answer_text: I have discussed the delivery of water and wastewater services with the Chair of the NI Water Board and both the Non-Executive and Executive Board Members.   I have provided NI Water with a total budget allocation of almost £0.5 billion, representing just under 40% of the total non-ring-fenced budget for my Department. This reflects the central importance of water and sewerage services to our society and to helping facilitate housing growth; I have discussed wastewater issues with Minister Lyons in respect of its impact on the delivery of the Housing Supply Strategy and advised that my Department is scoping out the potential for developers to contribute financially to increase the water and sewerage infrastructure capacity to help address wastewater issues, although this work is in the early stages.  I also highlighted the positive work that is being progressed by my Department through the Planning Improvement Programme, to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the planning system; andI have also discussed wastewater issues with a range of stakeholders including the Construction Employers Federation, Confederation of British Industry, Federation of Master Builders and the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors and outlined the measures being taken to help alleviate the impact of wastewater issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his Department's intention to review (i) Help with Health Costs; and (ii) the Hospital Travel Costs Scheme.
answer_text: My officials are currently finalising the policy options and a short public consultation will be conducted in the coming months on proposed changes to the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004, with the associated legislative process commencing in due course thereafter. As previously outlined (AQW 13474/22-27 refers), the focus of this work is to reinstate automatic passporting for those eligible to receive free sight tests and free Health Service dental treatment through the Help with Health Care (HwHC) Scheme following the migration of their benefits to Universal Credit (UC). The commencement of a wider review of the provisions within these Regulations and associated schemes will be considered once this urgent work is completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will consider a moratorium on the Housing Executive's Right to Buy Scheme.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer to the previous question AQW 7910/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his plans to introduce legislation to further implement the Reservoirs Act (Northern Ireland) 2015, to include powers to provide financial assistance to reservoir owners and managers.
answer_text: My Department is currently progressing five pieces of secondary legislation that will further commence and implement the Reservoirs Act (Northern Ireland) 2015. I plan to introduce this shortly.  This legislation will cover the main provisions of the Act and provides a permissive power to provide financial assistance to reservoir managers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the average processing time for blue badge (a) applications; and (b) renewals; (ii) the total number of successful applications in each of the last three years; and (iii) the measures being taken to improve these times, given the impact of delays on people with mobility issues.
answer_text: 1. (a) The average processing time for Blue Badge applications is currently 4 weeks for online applications and 5 weeks for paper applications. (b) The average processing time for renewals is included in the overall processing times listed above2. The total number of Blue Badges issued in each of the last three years:YearBadges Issued2021/2240,7742022/2351,5802023/2453,6993. The measures taken to improve these times are detailed below.My Department has announced a change in the renewal application process for Blue Badge holders with life-long mobility issues. Following a review, a shortened, more accessible application process has been introduced for these applicants if their qualifying Benefits Award or proven lifelong disability remained unchanged upon re-application. It applies to both online and paper applications. This new process reduces the need, in most cases, for the renewal application to be completely reassessed and has reduced processing times to four to five weeks. It is anticipated that this change will reduce the number of applications needing full reassessment by 65%.In June 2024 my Department introduced a change to the Blue Badge application process for those with “hidden disabilities”. This will permit such applications to be processed without the need for a GP referral thus reducing the processing time from months to weeks. My Department and the  Department for Communities have entered into an agreement to share information that will allow my Department to gather relevant information to support Blue Badge applications in a fraction of the time taken previously. About 55% of all Blue Badge applications are processed in this way.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will reconstitute the Interim Regional Planning Commission on a permanent basis to include members who engage with the planning system on a daily basis.
answer_text: The interim Regional Planning Commission’s composition has been designed to reflect the wide range of, often competing, interests in the planning system and as such I  have no immediate plans to reconstitute the group.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department plans to commission a large-scale Private Rented Sector Access Scheme.
answer_text: I currently have no plans to commission a large-scale Private Rented Sector Access Scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department will reconsider a ban on no fault evictions.
answer_text: I currently have no plans to ban no fault evictions. My Department is already taking forward work, as mandated by the Assembly, to increase security of tenure through the introduction of much longer notice to quit periods. My consideration of what further actions I can take to improve protections for tenants in this mandate will take account of what is happening in other jurisdictions, alongside the particular legislative and policy context for the Private Rented Sector here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the work of the Centre for Homelessness Impact on the Ending Homelessness Data Framework.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Since April 2024 the Housing Executive has facilitated a workshop with sectoral partners as part of ongoing work to develop a Framework. The Housing Executive continues to engage with the Centre for Homelessness Impact and a further workshop will be organised in October / November.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on (i) the H30 upgrade; and (ii) efforts to signalise the Caw roundabout, Derry.
answer_text: The upgrade and signalisation of Caw roundabout is included in the planning conditions for the adjacent H30 development and I can confirm that my officials continue to engage with the developer, South Bank Square on this matter. The developer has proposed changes to the infrastructure mitigations and delivery mechanism for Caw Roundabout, conditioned as part of the planning permission for the overall site, alongside additional information to justify their proposals.  On 20 September 2024 the developer submitted a report on their traffic assumptions and modelling updates.  This report is currently being reviewed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in light of the draft Programme for Government, to detail his Department's plans for a programme of reform in the Private Rented Sector.
answer_text: As set out in the draft Programme for Government, I will continue my programme of reform in the Private Rented Sector. Phase 1 of this reform is encompassed in the Private Tenancies Act (NI) 2022.  My Department has made good progress in implementing this legislation, including:Information to be provided to tenants and the requirement for receipts for cash payments, A limit on deposits and an increase in time to protect these deposits; andStandards for Smoke, Heat and Carbon Monoxide Alarms. Subject to Assembly approval, it is anticipated that Regulations on Electrical Safety Standards and Notice to Quit Exceptions will be in place in the coming months. Regulations on Minimum Energy Efficiency standards remain at early consideration stage.  Given their complexity and cross cutting nature, it is likely that they will take at least 2-3 years to complete.		My Department has commenced work to transfer the operation of Landlord Registration to Councils. The aim of this work is to make registration a more meaningful tool to improve the Private Rented Sector. It is anticipated that the transfer will commence next year on a phased basis.	I am currently considering what further actions I can take in Phase 2 of my programme that will further improve the safety, security and standards of the Sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the spend per capita on public transport in each of the last three years, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: The Department’s main budget for public transport is allocated on a regional basis to Translink. In addition, Translink do not record the utilisation of this budget by local council area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in,light of the commitment in the draft Programme for Government 2024-2027, to detail the support he will provide City of Derry Airport to develop new routes.
answer_text: I welcome the commitment given in the Programme for Government to look at how we can develop new routes and offer further support to our three airports to support tourism and deliver economic growth.My Department commissioned the Report “Air Connectivity to Support Inbound Tourism and Business Growth” which was published in March 2024. Departmental officials met recently with the management team at the City of Derry Airport to discuss the Report’s recommendations for strategic routes and policy levers to support our air connectivity. My Officials will work collaboratively with the aviation sector to identify opportunities for route development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of children that were living in temporary accommodation on (i) 1 January; and (ii) 1 September, in each of the last five years, broken down by (a) age; (b) sex; (c) Assembly constituency; and (d) local council area.
answer_text: The biannual Northern Ireland Homelessness Bulletin October 2023 – March 2024 contains information on a range of areas relating to homelessness and is available at https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/publications/northern-ireland-homelessness-bulletin-october-2023-march-2024  	Tables 3.3 and 3.4 provide details on children placed in temporary accommodation by type of accommodation and age category. As the information is not published in the format you have requested, I am unable to provide you with a more detailed response.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the number of new social housing units built in rural areas in each of the last five years; and (ii) the percentage of the Social Housing Development Programme this constituted.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“In the last five years a total of 491 homes have been built in Rural Areas. The table below breaks down the detail of these units.Year1 Bed2 Bed3 Bed4 BedTotalPercentage2019/2010504511066.52%2020/21157849414611.20%2021/2282081374.43%2022/2364595654.49%2023/247755411379.76%”

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the number of units of temporary accommodation in use on 1 September were in rural areas; and (ii) the percentage this equates to of the total temporary accommodation stock.
answer_text: The biannual Northern Ireland Homelessness Bulletin October 2023 – March 2024 contains information on a range of areas relating to homelessness and is available at https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/publications/northern-ireland-homelessness-bulletin-october-2023-march-2024   	Section 3 provides details on Temporary Accommodation however the Housing Executive has advised it does not categorise temporary accommodation by urban or rural areas, and it is therefore not possible to provide this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the total underspend returned to Treasury by the Northern Ireland Road Safety Partnership in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The total underspend returned to HM Treasury by the Northern Ireland Road Safety Partnership in each of the last 5 years amounts is as follows:Year endedTotal underspend (fee and fine income) returned, £000s31/03/202445931/03/202391831/03/202283631/03/202167631/03/2020351TOTAL3,240You may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Road Safety Partnership for further information.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on (i) efforts to secure a dedicated paediatric pathology service for Northern Ireland; and (ii) the exploration of cross-border collaboration on this matter.
answer_text: As I noted in the Assembly debate on cross-border healthcare on 18 June 2024, the prospect of a dedicated paediatric pathology service for Northern Ireland is very remote, as these services are under considerable pressure across the UK and globally due to a shortage of paediatric pathologists.  As these services move to more centralised models nationally, my first priority is to ensure that we maintain access to high-quality, reliable and timely pathology services at Alder Hey, and I am assured that we have been able to secure access by further extending these arrangements that have worked well since 2019.  I also noted that I am keen to explore the feasibility of an all-island approach to these services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when the Centre for Homelessness Impact’s report on Value for Money in respect of temporary accommodation will be published.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The report is currently in draft format and the Housing Executive continues to work closely with the Centre for Homelessness Impact in the development of a final report.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 10879/22-27, and in relation to the exercise that was conducted in November 2023, to provide clarity on (i) what this process entailed; (ii) how many non-standard properties were involved, broken down by council area; and (iii) when a repeat exercise will take place.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive and been advised that:“The inspections carried out in November 2023 were to ensure non-standard accommodation was being used by those placed in such accommodation. As per AQW 12948 the exercise did not identify any issues in terms of occupancy or quality of non-standard accommodation and therefore 100% of properties were deemed reasonable.A total of 41 distinct premises were visited in October/November 2023 with the table below providing a breakdown of the number of premises by Council area.Council AreaNumber of visitsAntrim and Newtownabbey4Belfast4Causeway Coast and Glens4Derry and Strabane22Fermanagh and Omagh 5Mid and East Antrim 1Newry, Mourne and Down1Further to the exercise conducted in November 2023, a repeat exercise was conducted in July/August 2024.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline the findings from the independent review into the Department’s approach to the Peacemakers Museum, Foyle.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his plans in terms of North South collaboration on road safety enhancements.
answer_text: As reflected in my Department’s recently launched Road Safety Strategy 2030, we will continue to work with colleagues in the south/Irish Government to tackle the death toll across the island and to address any cross-border road engineering and speed concerns.  Road safety is regularly discussed at the North South Ministerial Council - Transport Meetings where opportunities for effective collaboration on proposed cross-border initiatives are considered. On the Executive Flagship A5 dual carriageway project, which would, on completion, make a very significant contribution to road safety, my officials work in close collaboration with counterparts in Donegal County Council in relation to their proposed TEN-T road improvement projects and in Monaghan County Council in relation to their road improvements projects along the adjoining N2.  My Department also has plans to improve road safety along a 25km stretch of the A1 dual carriageway extending from Hillsborough Roundabout to Loughbrickland.  This scheme will close 100 gaps in the central median and provide 4 new grade separated junctions and deliver significant safety enhancements along this important North South road corridor.My Department is also preparing a new suite of Transport Plans which will set the framework for transport policy and investment decisions up until 2035.  A Public Engagement exercise on the proposed Vision and Objectives for the North West Transport Plan is currently ongoing.  This includes an objective on making improvements in transport safety and reducing road casualties.  As part of the engagement, officials have met with Donegal County Council to seek their input and feedback.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, as part of the decision on Winter Fuel Payments, to detail (i) whether his Department has carried out an assessment on (a) the impact on pensioners entitled to, but not claiming, Pension Credit; (b) the impact on pensioners' health; and (ii) any alternative support that is being considered by his Department.
answer_text: In view of the unexpected and urgent nature of the announcement by the Chancellor, there has not been sufficient time for my department to conduct specific research of the potential impact in Northern Ireland of the changes to the Winter Fuel Payment eligibility criteria. A full Equality Impact Assessment will now be progressed by my Department in the coming weeks.Given the Barnett consequential arising from the announcement on Monday 2 September 2024 by Work and Pensions Secretary, Liz Kendall MP, to extend the Household Support Fund in GB, my Department will be seeking funding to help struggling households with bills and essential costs over winter. I want to ensure that any additional money allocated to the NI Block will be used to ensure that vulnerable older people in Northern Ireland receive assistance with essential costs this winter. I am keen to explore, along with Executive colleagues, the option (with the help of DWP) of issuing a one-off hardship payment to those in receipt of state pension who will lose out on the Winter Fuel Payment this winter.My officials are currently working with DWP counterparts to consider the legislative and operational implications of this approach. I will keep the Assembly informed about how we can best help in this regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on a baby loss certificate scheme.
answer_text: I am keen to see this scheme taken forward as quickly as possible to give bereaved families the opportunity to have formal recognition of their loss.I met with the Health Minister last week to receive an update on progress from General Register Office officials in my Department who are working closely with the Department of Health officials on how the baby loss scheme work may be developed.My officials and those from the Department of Health have met with managers from the Department of Health and Social Care in England and with the National Records of Scotland officials to learn more about how their schemes operate. This ongoing engagement will provide important lessons for the development of a scheme here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) whether he will put plans in place to restore the Hardship Fund for local councils; and (ii) any engagement he has had with Executive colleagues in relation to ensuring the Hardship Fund is an Executive funding priority moving forward.
answer_text: The funding available for my Department was set within the context of a constrained financial position for 2024/25, and the overall Departmental budget position was considered against competing priorities. Due to the financial constraints on the Department’s budget the Department is currently unable to provide any hardship funding for 2024/25 and due to the continuing uncertainty in funding it would not be possible to consider a recurrent funding model at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the number of pension households currently entitled to but not claiming Pension Credit; and (ii) whether he will consider a specific pension outreach programme to encourage uptake of this benefit.
answer_text: This Department’s most recently published statistics on the uptake of Pension Credit were released on 01 September 2022 and provided estimates for the 2019/20 financial year.  The estimates indicate that 72% of eligible families (66,300) were in receipt of Pension Credit, while 28% (26,300) of families with potential entitlement to Pension Credit were not receiving it.My Department undertakes a range of activities to promote Pension Credit uptake including: regular social media and broadcast campaigns; distribution of Pension Credit information leaflets to libraries and GP surgeries; working with voluntary and community stakeholders to raise awareness of potential benefit entitlement; supporting pensioners by completing confidential benefit entitlement assessments and providing help with form completion; and discussing Pension Credit entitlement when a person makes their State Retirement Pension claim. My Department is committed to supporting older people to access the benefits they are entitled to, for example through the Make the Call service, and will continue to seek opportunities to encourage Pension Credit uptake.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she will address gaps in Section 44 of the The Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 2008, to ensure same-sex couples who conceive through IVF treatment conducted outside of the UK can both be legally recognised as their child's parents.
answer_text: The 2008 Act is a reserved matter, and therefore lies outside the legislative competence of both my Department and the Assembly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the (i) Anti-Poverty Strategy; and (ii) Child Poverty Strategy.
answer_text: Addressing poverty in all its forms is a key priority for me. I am committed to working with all my Ministerial colleagues to ensure that the Executive’s Anti-Poverty Strategy is taken forward in the most effective and expeditious way possible, taking account of departmental budget allocations and the need to develop a prioritised, sustainable and deliverable Strategy action plan.I am currently considering the next steps to be taken in relation to the Anti-Poverty Strategy and addressing the issues faced by children and young people remains a key focus of this ongoing work. I will outline my proposed next steps on Strategy development to my Executive colleagues in the coming weeks. Final decisions on the content and implementation of the Anti-Poverty Strategy will be subject to Executive agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for an update on the extension of welfare mitigations, including the Benefit Cap mitigation; and (ii) for his assurances that this extension will be implemented before the March 2025 deadline.
answer_text: My Department has a statutory obligation to produce a report on the operation of the existing Welfare Supplementary Payment schemes before 31 March 2025.I am committed to laying this report before the Assembly by the end of 2024, this will allow time for the introduction of any legislation that may be required ahead of 31 March 2025.It will be for the Executive to consider and agree any future mitigation package to ensure that the limited resources available are used in the most effective way possible to provide support to those in need.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any further consideration he has given to introducing a universal breakfast club scheme for primary schools.
answer_text: Given the current funding pressures faced by the education sector, I have no immediate plans to introduce a universal breakfast scheme for primary schools.The Department of Education has provided funding to schools via the Extended Schools programme since 2006, representing an investment of more than £170m. 450 schools will benefit from this investment this year and schools have the flexibility to determine how best to deploy those resources. Activities include breakfast or homework clubs, sport, art, drama, digital skills, parental engagement, community use of school premises and so on. Schools which are not eligible for Extended Schools funding can choose to run their own activities and seek sponsorship through programmes funded by other organisations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of people that have used the Dental Access Scheme in the last two years, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: A Northern Ireland-wide Dental Access Scheme (DAS) commenced on 1st August 2024.  This new scheme aims to improve access (during the working week) for unregistered patients experiencing an emergency, urgent, or pressing dental condition.  There are currently 28 practices participating in the scheme and over 1200 patients were seen as part of the scheme in its first four weeks.  It is not possible, at this point in time, to provide a break-down of the patients seen at constituency level.  Details of the scheme (including participating practices) can be viewed at:  https://online.hscni.net/dental-access-scheme/

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding being distributed to each local council area to deliver the social supermarkets programme.
answer_text: My Department has allocated the following amounts for each Council area to support Social Supermarket activity in 2024/25: Council AreaSocial Supermarket Total 24/25 Allocation Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council£162,746Ards and North Down Borough Council£127,822Armagh City, Banbridge and Craigavon Borough Council£253,905Belfast City Council£731,091Causeway Coast and Glens Borough Council£160,590Derry City and Strabane District Council£376,214                                      Fermanagh and Omagh District Council £166,307Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council£194,897Mid and East Antrim Borough Council£159,800Mid Ulster District Council £103,603Newry, Mourne and Down District Council£181,247

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will consider working with the logistics industry to develop a cross-Executive logistics strategy to help unlock the full economic potential of this sector and workforce.
answer_text: I consider the logistics industry to be an essential component of a healthy economy and my officials maintain regular contacts with, and provide assistance to, the local trade representatives. Whilst my Department has responsibility for operator licencing, driver licencing, vehicle standards, and, through DVA, enforcement and testing of vehicles, responsibilities for other areas of the logistics sector lie within other Departments. I cannot speak for those Departments but I would welcome the opportunity to work more closely on this subject with other members of the Executive.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on work to establish an Infrastructure Commission, as committed to in the Executive’s Covid Recovery Strategy.
answer_text: The SIB Review is currently being finalised and will help to inform a revised paper on options for an Infrastructure Commission.  Decisions on the way forward will be a matter for the Executive in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans he has to progress rail freight, given that a single freight train can remove up to 129 heavy goods vehicles from the road at a time, therefore helping to reduce congestion on the road network and reduce carbon emissions in line with the Executive’s obligations under the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: I am keen that we are ambitious around our rail network and maximise the potential of rail to support social and economic development and the environmental sustainability of our transport system. Rail freight has an important role to play in this and increasing rail freight’s market share is one of the objectives of the All-Island Strategic Rail Review.The delivery of interventions in the Review, such as increasing capacity and improving the infrastructure of our existing corridors; building the proposed new rail links from Portadown to Mullingar and to Derry; and developing a network of inland freight terminals, will be necessary to support the development of rail freight in the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount spent on temporary accommodation in the last 12 months, broken down by local council area.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The following tables detail the amount spent on temporary accommodation in the 2023/24 financial year. Table 1 This table details expenditure on all forms of temporary accommodation per Local Council area, where it has been possible to identify this.Temporary Accommodation (£)Antrim & Newtownabbey514,111Ards & North Down841,777Armagh, Banbridge & Craigavon839,626Belfast6,283,239Causeway Coast & Glens290,174Derry & Strabane6,552,009Fermanagh & Omagh555,263Lisburn & Castlereagh884,317Mid & East Antrim240,940Mid Ulster374,101Newry, Mourne & Down802,500Costs by Council area18,178,056Table 2This table represents temporary accommodation which is located in the Belfast Council area only.  Unlike Table 1, it represents the Council where the applicant was placed (as opposed to the area where the applicant presented) – although there would be a strong correlation.  These costs are additional to the Belfast Council costs included in Table 1.Temporary Accommodation (£) Farset 614,019Block Booking Non-Standard1,043,241Queens Quarter Housing99,645Belfast Accommodation costs1,756,904Table 3It should also be noted that within temporary accommodation costs there are additional categories of spend which cannot be broken down by Council Area at present.Temporary Accommodation (£) Block Booked Single lets14,073,419Miscellaneous 386,699Centralised Costs14,460,118Total temporary accommodation costs for 2023/24 were £34,395,078.The figures provided are the gross cost to NIHE of providing temporary accommodation and excludes any Housing Benefit or personal contributions received by NIHE on behalf of the occupant.The above centralised costs (Table 3) are currently under review as Block Booked Single Let costs are centrally managed on a separate system which does not currently allow for costs to be recorded by Council area or presenting office. A further category of ‘Miscellaneous’ is included - this relates to all placements and costs where information relating to Council Area are not captured at source on NIHE systems. Again, it is not possible to readily provide this split across various Local Council areas.” For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 10123/22-27, whether he has considered the findings of the review of residents' parking schemes.
answer_text: The review of residents’ parking schemes is complete, and I have had the opportunity to consider the draft review report and I plan to issue the final review report soon.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the measures his Department is taking to address the growing issue of polysubstance abuse, addiction and mental health challenges in the homeless population.
answer_text: The issues of homelessness, involvement with the justice system, mental health and substance use are all inter-related and span the responsibilities of several departments. People experiencing homelessness often have complex and intersecting physical and mental health needs, drug and alcohol recovery needs, and social care needs that may be contributing factors to becoming homeless as well as consequences of homelessness.While officials from my Department currently participate in a number of cross-departmental and stakeholder working groups focused on improving the lives or life chances of those experiencing homelessness, the key group is the Homelessness Strategy Steering Group (HSSG). This leads the implementation of the NIHE’s Ending Homelessness Together: Homelessness Strategy for Northern Ireland 2022-27 and annual Inter-departmental Homelessness Action Plans (IDHAP). This focuses on addressing gaps in ‘non-accommodation’ services beyond the remit of the Housing Executive. DoH responsibilities in the IDHAP mainly relate to Mental Health Strategy actions relating to early intervention and prevention; the continued development of the Belfast Inclusion Health Services (a hub for homeless services); new drugs and alcohol services; dual diagnosis support and the domestic and sexual violence helpline. Officials are currently engaging regarding the development of actions for the next iteration of the IDHAP.In respect of substance use, addressing the harm related to alcohol and other drugs is a key priority for my Department. NI’s Substance Use Strategy, ‘Preventing Harm, Empowering Recovery,’ was published in September 2021 following approval from the then Executive. The Strategy recognises the need to target those most at risk, including those who are homeless, people who inject drugs, and those who are in contact with the justice system. It aims to directly reduce harm for these groups. It is important to note there are a range of substance use services available across Northern Ireland, full details of the services available in each area can be found at: https://drugsandalcoholni.info/self-help-resources/. A new strategic plan for the commissioning of substance use services will be published soon, and it recognises the issue faced by those experiencing homelessness. The full implementation of this plan will be dependent on available resources, but we will also look to re-design existing services to improve outcomes.A further development in this area is the Complex Lives initiative established by the Public Health Agency, Belfast City Council, and the Housing Executive, which is operating and providing support to those who are homeless and at risk in the Belfast area. With a Multi-Disciplinary Team in place, it is providing a whole system, accountable care approach to supporting people experiencing multiple disadvantages with the aim of helping people engage, stabilise, and find a new way forward with their lives. The initiative involves all key

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the steps being taken to reduce (i) the costs associated with; and (ii) the reliance upon, emergency accommodation, particularly in areas such as Derry.
answer_text: The Housing Executive has statutory responsibility for responding to homelessness, including provision of homelessness accommodation and has informed me it recently increased its focus on reducing non-standard placements in the Derry City and Strabane District Council area.  My officials continue to engage with the Housing Executive to identify specific actions which can be taken to control the use of, and consequent spend on, temporary accommodation. Business cases in relation to more financially sustainable options for the provision of temporary accommodation are currently being considered. I am also looking at a broad range of initiatives to address the wider issue of housing provision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will work with Executive colleagues to secure funding for Foyle Haven Day Centre and other homeless support groups.
answer_text: My Department is facing a challenging budget position. I have already taken the decision to protect funding for Homelessness services at last year’s level, however this budget is under pressure.  	The consultation on the Equality Impact Assessment for my 2024/25 budget closed on 3 September and responses are currently being considered before final decisions are taken with regards funding. Any future actions by DfC for Homelessness services will be subject to available budget.	I will continue to make the case for more funding for housing and homelessness.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he has engaged with his Executive colleagues around the potential reintroduction of the Republic of Ireland Reimbursement Scheme.
answer_text: I am deeply invested in effective engagement and collaboration with my Executive colleagues on areas such as waiting list initiatives amongst many others to deliver better outcomes for the people of Northern Ireland. However, under the 2024/25 Budget approved by the Executive, no additional funding has been provided for tackling our waiting lists. A bid for funding for a replacement scheme was submitted to the Department of Finance during this year’s budget setting process. The Reimbursement Scheme bid of £31million was part of a wider bid for £135million of additional funding to tackle waiting lists. It was intended that a new reimbursement scheme could have treated over 4,000 patients this financial year. This was in addition to a separate bid of £53million, within the wider £135million, specifically to address some 4,400 patients waiting over 2 years for orthopaedic procedures. Unfortunately, instead of now being able to invest in such waiting list initiatives, my Department’s budget allocation for 2024/25 represents a 2.3% reduction in funding compared to our expenditure in 2023/24. This is a deeply regrettable and concerning position. As such, there is insufficient funding at present to reinstate a cross-border reimbursement scheme. The financial pressures across the Health and Social Care (HSC) system are certainly severe and the 2024/25 budget outcome falls far short of what is needed to tackle the unacceptable waiting lists. Therefore, my Department’s focus is on supporting those on suspect cancer and time critical waiting lists.While the £121m received in the June 2024/25 monitoring round makes some contribution to reducing the deficit position facing the HSC system, a significant shortfall still remains. As some £75m of this had already been factored into our 2024-25 financial plan, the remaining £46m will reduce the HSC Trust funding shortfall to £143m with a total gap of £293m. The financial position therefore remains very challenging and work continues across the HSC system to deliver the maximum possible savings and efficiency.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the options being considered to make Ebrington Square a viable and attractive venue for concerts.
answer_text: Ebrington supports many creative, cultural, sporting, charitable and other community-led events and activities. It also provides a high-quality outdoor space for music events, in addition to other venues and locations across the city. We continue to work in partnership with Derry City and Strabane District Council (DCSDC) and other stakeholders to maximise the social, economic and cultural benefits of the entire Ebrington site.  A call for Expressions of Interest for large scale licensed events in both 2025 and 2026 has recently closed.  The application process was adapted in response to feedback to ensure longer lead in times for event promoters and reduce administrative requirements.  Hire fees for the site have also been frozen for this period.  Applications are currently being assessed by DCSDC and interest indicates Ebrington remains viable and attractive for event promoters. TEO and Council officials will continue to engage with promoters locally and elsewhere, and strengthen a city-wide approach to music, cultural and other creative events which can showcase Derry~Londonderry and the North West. TEO has also engaged a local marketing company to enhance promotion of Ebrington as an attractive venue. In support of this, a series of community workshops were held during June 2024 to enable people to share ideas on ways to develop Ebrington. These insights will be shared in the weeks ahead and will inform next steps to ensure Ebrington is used year-round for large events, concerts and other family friendly and community activities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline what measures he plans to put in place to financially support pensioners who will lose their Winter Fuel Payments as a result of changes to the scheme.
answer_text: I strongly disagree with, and I am totally opposed to the Labour Government’s decision to change the Winter Fuel Payment eligibility criteria and I am very concerned about the serious consequences this will have on the comfort, wellbeing and health of older people across Northern Ireland.Given the Barnett consequential arising from the announcement on Monday 2 September 2024 by Work and Pensions Secretary, Liz Kendall MP, to extend the Household Support Fund in GB, my Department will be seeking funding to help struggling households with bills and essential costs over winter. I want to ensure that any additional money allocated to the NI Block will be used to ensure that vulnerable older people in Northern Ireland receive assistance with essential costs this winter. I am keen to explore, along with Executive colleagues, the option (with the help of DWP) of issuing a one-off hardship payment to those in receipt of state pension who will lose out on the Winter Fuel Payment this winter.My officials are currently working with DWP counterparts to consider the legislative and operational implications of this approach. I will keep the Assembly informed about how we can best help in this regard.In the meantime, it is extremely important that people ensure that they are receiving all of the benefits they are entitled to. I would therefore encourage everyone to check their entitlement to Pension Credit at their earliest opportunity. This can be done by telephoning the Northern Ireland Pension Centre’s Pension Credit Application Line.The Department’s unique Make the Call Wraparound Service puts people in touch with any benefits, supports and services to which they may be entitled, and I would also encourage people to get in touch with them.My officials are also working on a Fuel Poverty Strategy that will provide a long-term framework for reducing fuel poverty, recognising that this will have positive impacts on both mental and physical health, especially for the most vulnerable in society. The strategy will address issues such as improving energy efficiency in homes, a new fuel poverty energy efficiency scheme, how to support those living in or at risk of fuel poverty and ensure consumer protection.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following the passage of the Defective Premises Bill, to detail the number of homes that have been identified as being impacted by defective blocks.
answer_text: To date, six social houses have been identified as being impacted by MICA affected blocks.  These houses have since been remediated.  Additionally, one private dwelling has been identified as being impacted by MICA affected blocks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of families impacted by the two-child benefit limit.
answer_text: In May 2024, there were 10,830 households receiving Universal Credit impacted by the two-child policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on his Department's engagement with the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs in relation to the progress of the Drumahoe to Caw phase of the A6.
answer_text: As you will be aware, part of the works for A6 Phase 2 encroach on the illegal Mobuoy waste site and the final design will need to take this into account. My Department continues to work collaboratively with the NIEA and other stakeholders, to progress the remediation ofthis site. This integrated approach will ensure that any proposals take account of each parties’ requirements, including those associated with the A6.My officials are currently waiting on DAERA to confirm their Optimum Remediation Strategy and provide a programme for the remediation of the Mobuoy site. Once this information is provided, my Department will then be able to develop a delivery strategy, including project timeline, for progressing A6 Phase 2.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will address the lack of Irish language signage at Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: I am committed to the visibility and promotion of Irish Language across public services and in our society.  I am continuing to engage with Translink in relation to Irish language signage at Grand Central Station and I’m confident that the matter can be resolved.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he has met with the Education Authority to ascertain why there are children awaiting a school placement for this academic year.
To ask the Minister of Health whether he will engage with Executive colleagues to secure funding for the Men's Action Network in Foyle.
answer_text: It would not be appropriate for me to engage with Executive colleagues to advocate on behalf of one particular group for future funding. The 2024/25 Health Budget allocation was a hugely disappointing outcome for the health and social care system here. Both my predecessor and I robustly made the case for greater recognition of the challenges facing patients, staff, and organisations, yet regrettably a majority of MLAs chose to vote for a budget that without doubt is going to cause harm, as well as limit any opportunity to support a wide range of otherwise essential projects moving forward.Given the very significant financial challenges my Department is currently experiencing I regret to advise that I simply do not have any available funding I could offer to MAN. Department of Finance are dealing with an associated dormant funds request as that is outside the responsibility of this Department.There will be many organisations who would wish to make a case for funding but given the financial position I am currently not able to offer support, particularly in advance of a clear future budget position for the services currently funded by my Department.In Health, all the available funding is directed to the areas of greatest need. Within the budget we have been allocated, our priority has been to minimise the impact on front line services and we will have to continue to focus on this for the foreseeable future.I would stress that I value the important work carried out by MAN and indeed the wider Voluntary and Community Sector (VCS) to improve health and social care outcomes for the people of Northern Ireland. For this reason, I want to address the fundamental unfairness in the current Core Grant scheme, which has funded the same organisations for more than 20 years.The Department will be working with the VCS, facilitated by NICVA, to redesign the scheme to ensure maximum value for the sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she will allow businesses in Foyle Street, Derry, to benefit from a reduction in their Rates bill due to the prolonged closure of the street to facilitate major works by NI Water.
answer_text: An application from an affected ratepayer of Foyle Street has been received by the District Valuer, who is considering if a temporary allowance is warranted.  The rating system here does not provide automatic relief in circumstances of public realm or infrastructure works. Each instance is assessed on its own merits considering the impact of works on the Net Annual Value (NAV) of the property. The outcome of the application, and any others received, will inform if a wider review is needed of similarly affected property in Foyle Street.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the dualling of the A2 Buncrana Road.
answer_text: Work on the A2 Buncrana Road scheme was paused through a decision taken under the NI (Executive Formation, etc) Act 2022, to allow appropriate alignment with emerging Transport Plans. The A2 will be considered as part of the North West Transport Plan, which is at an early stage of development. Views from the public are currently being sought to help shape the vision and objectives for the Transport Plan, as well as identifying local transport concerns in the district. Public engagement commenced on 27th August 2024 and will run until 5th November.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail his plans to mitigate against the impact of the withdrawal of the Winter Fuel Payment from some pension households.
answer_text: Given the Barnett consequential arising from the announcement on Monday 2 September 2024 by Work and Pensions Secretary, Liz Kendall MP, to extend the Household Support Fund in GB, I am seeking Executive colleagues’ agreement that any additional money allocated to the NI Block should be provided to my department. This will help ensure that vulnerable older people in Northern Ireland receive assistance with essential costs this winter.In the meantime, it is extremely important that people ensure they are receiving all of the benefits they are entitled to. I would therefore encourage everyone to check their entitlement to Pension Credit at their earliest opportunity. This can be done by phoning the Northern Ireland Pension Centre’s Pension Credit Application Line.The Department’s unique Make the Call Wraparound Service puts people in touch with any benefits, supports and services to which they may be entitled, and I would also encourage people to get in touch with them.My officials are also working on a Fuel Poverty Strategy that will provide a long-term framework for reducing fuel poverty, recognising that this will have positive impacts on both mental and physical health, especially for the most vulnerable in society.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his plans to secure funding for NI Water to facilitate the development of homes.
answer_text: I have provided NI Water with a total budget allocation of almost £0.5 billion in 2024-25, representing just under 40% of the total non-ring-fenced budget for my Department. This reflects the central importance of water and sewerage services to our society.In terms of assisting connections to the wastewater network, my Department encourages developers and wastewater management professionals to use Sustainable Drainage Systems (SuDS) to help remove surface water from the sewer network and free up capacity for wastewater. This can also reduce flooding and pollution risks. In addition, NI Water asks developers and wastewater management professionals to engage with it early, through the Pre- Development Enquiry process, to try and find a resolution to any issues at an early stage, to help facilitate development, where possible.My Department has also begun looking into the feasibility of contributions from developers towards upgrades to wastewater treatment works and sewerage infrastructure to facilitate development. This work is in the very early stages and further investigations with the relevant stakeholders are required to progress this matter.NI Water  advises that even if NI Water’s PC21 programme was fully funded, it would still only be possible to address 4,500 of these 19,000 units, as the solutions to capacity constraints will take several fully funded Price Control periods to resolve.We all want to see a sustainable water and wastewater infrastructure which benefits everyone across our society; therefore, I will continue to raise the need for NI Water, to have secure and sustainable funding, with the NI Executive and the British Government, to help deliver the necessary infrastructure to enable the building of homes for all our people.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the organisations and projects funded by his Department to specifically support men's mental health.
answer_text: Mental health services for men are generally provided on a universal basis irrespective of gender. Most services are open to all genders and this would include Lifeline and the Self Harm Improvement Programme (SHIP). question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how his Department's June monitoring round allocation of £12.5 million for critical infrastructure projects will be spent.
answer_text: I am currently considering the outcome of June Monitoring for my department following the additional resource and capital budget provided. I will be considering this along with the  equality responses received within the first four weeks of my Department’s Budget 2024-2025 Equality Impact Assessment consultation to inform my final opening budget decisions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of NHS patients registered with a dental practice in each of the last five years.
answer_text: The number of Health Service patients registered with Health Service dental practices in Northern Ireland in each of the last five years is shown in Table 1 below.  Table 1 Number of paid registrations by year, 2020 – 20241YearDental Patient Registrations20201,219,10320211,246,21320221,289,11020231,341,97420241,297,141Source: Family Practitioner Services (FPS) Dental Payment System, HSC Business Services Organisation1 Dentists receive a payment for each patient registered. Figures relate to those registrations paid in March of each year e.g. the 2024 figure relates to paid registrations in March 2024.This information is routinely published as part of the Accredited Official Statistics release ‘FPS General Dental Services for Northern Ireland, Annual Series’.  This report and quarterly updates within a year are published at General Dental Services Statistics - Business Services Organisation (BSO) Website (hscni.net). Quarterly updates should be treated as provisional until such times as the year-end and Quarter 4 figures are released, when the statistics are finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the current staff levels in his Department’s planning section, including the current number of vacant positions.
answer_text: There are currently 62.2 FTE staff within DfI Planning, and there are 16 vacancies.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on plans to increase the number of staff focused on renewable planning applications in his Department’s planning section, given the legislative requirements on net zero targets.
answer_text: My Department is looking to fill a range of vacancies in the Planning Group, in line with my Department’s priorities including renewable energy planning applications, and which will be subject to the availability of the necessary funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the current number of departmental officials who specialise in managing renewable energy planning applications.
answer_text: At present, the Department has six planners involved in the processing of renewable planning applications, although given other Departmental work, they do not work exclusively on renewable planning applications.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the total funding contribution from the Executive for the A5 in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: The total funding contribution provided to my Department for the A5 in the 2024-25 financial year was £88.5m, as agreed by the Executive as part of the overall total Capital DEL funding available.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the total number of complaints received by their Department requesting improved cleaning and maintenance of Ebrington Square in each of the last three years.
answer_text: The Executive Office has received correspondence on cleaning and maintenance of Ebrington Square in each of the last three years as follows:   1 July 2021 - 30 June 20221 July 2022 - 30 June 20231 July 2023 – 30 June 2024104We want to provide reassurance that we have a significant maintenance and cleaning schedule in place at Ebrington Square.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for a breakdown of how his Department's £10 million allocation as part of the June monitoring round will be spent.
answer_text: The £10m Resource allocation my Department received in June Monitoring, only offsets pressures already faced, particularly with regards demand-led Homelessness, Supporting People and Discretionary Support.  Further resource funding is critical, or the actions that my Department is required to take to live within budget will adversely impact services to the most vulnerable in our society. The actions required to live within my Department’s Budget are subject to Equality Impact Assessment consultation which launched on 11 June 2024.  The consultation is available on the Department's internet page, or via the following link -https://www.communities-ni.gov.uk/consultations/consultation-department-communities-budget-2024-25-allocations

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to provide clarity on (i) the non-consolidated recognition payment in lieu of back pay for eligible school support staff 2025-26; and (ii) when he will be in a position to confirm this provision.
answer_text: In the proposed agreement developed with support staff unions, the non-consolidated payment (NCP) of £2,550 will be made to staff who were in post on 1 April 2024 and will be pro-rata according to actual hours worked during the 2023-24 financial year.  The payment will therefore be reduced proportionately for staff who worked part time hours.Further details on this payment can be found at https://www.education-ni.gov.uk/articles/pay-related-issues.The Executive has committed to provide funding for the non-consolidated payment in April 2025, if HM Treasury has not provided funding to allow implementation of the Pay and Grading Review in full before that date.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of tooth extractions carried out in hospitals for patients under the age of 18 in each of the last five years, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The table below shows the number of tooth extractions carried out in hospitals for patients under the age of 18, broken down by Health and Social Care trust, in each of the last five financial years.Table 1.
