answer_text: The upgrade and signalisation of the Caw Roundabout is a planning condition of the development at H30, which is to be constructed prior to the occupation of the 301st dwelling. This upgrade is required to provide mitigation against the increased traffic flow resulting from construction of the new housing development.In addition to this upgrade, my Department is considering further enhancements to the roundabout which would also improve overall safety and congestion issues at the site.Ideally it would be beneficial to deliver both the H30 and Department upgrades as a single project, to minimise disruption to road users.  However, given the significant funding pressures currently faced by my Department, there is currently no funding available to complete the proposed DfI upgrades and my consideration of future funding for such works will be informed by the transport priorities identified by the Regional Strategic Transport Network Transport Plan.  Therefore, the delivery of the H30 upgrade proposals at Caw Roundabout as a separate project currently seems the most viable option.Discussions are ongoing between departmental officials and the developer to agree the timeframe for the delivery of the H30 upgrade to Caw Roundabout.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to outline what steps have been taken to ensure full time students, particularly parents with young children, can access financial support including the Adviser Discretion Fund.
answer_text: My officials are engaging with representatives across the further and higher education network in Northern Ireland with the aim of increasing awareness of the financial provisions available to full time students.  Information on all Benefits support available to students can be accessed online via www.nidirect.gov.uk/articles/benefits-higher-education-students. This includes signposting to Universal Credit for those full-time students who are eligible for help with housing costs, children, childcare, etc. Everyone making a new claim to Universal Credit will receive a welcome message which includes a link to the range of finance support my Department offers, including the Universal Credit contingency fund grant.  This non-repayable grant provides help with living expenses to those facing financial difficulty while waiting for your first full Universal Credit payment. Further details are available via www.nidirect.gov.uk/campaigns/finance-support or by calling Freephone 0800 587 2750. The Adviser Discretion Fund (ADF) is a non-repayable grant award of up to £1500 within a twelve-month period which may be used to buy goods or services to remove barriers to employment, such as help towards training costs, the cost of travelling to work or to a job interview and importantly help with upfront childcare costs in certain circumstances. Students can check if they are eligible for the ADF by speaking to a Work Coach in their local Jobs & Benefits office. Eligibility information can be found at www.nidirect.gov.uk/adf

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether she has had any engagement with the Minister of Health to ensure that students in receipt of nursing bursaries can access means tested benefits.
answer_text: My Department has not had any recent engagement with the Minister of Health about students in receipt of nursing bursaries and access to means tested benefits. It is no longer possible to make a new claim to income-related legacy benefits including Income Support, Income-based Jobseeker’s Allowance, Income-related Employment and Support Allowance and Housing Benefit.  For students who are currently in receipt of a nursing bursary and any of these income-related benefits, their bursary is treated as grant income and is taken into account when calculating entitlement to these benefits, subject to any disregards that may apply.When a nursing student in receipt of a bursary makes a claim to Universal Credit the bursary is disregarded, except for any specific amount in the bursary which covers rent payments (if an amount is included in the Universal Credit award for housing costs) or payments for dependents who are included in the Universal Credit award.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he has had any engagement with the Minister of Communities to ensure that students in receipt of nursing bursaries can access means tested benefits.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of Occupational Therapists employed by each Health and Social Care Trust, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: Information on the number of occupational therapists employed in each Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust at 31st March 2022, is detailed in the table below.At 31st March 2022BelfastNorthernSouth EasternSouthernWesternOccupational therapists employed#327 (300.2 WTE)304(256.5 WTE)239 (219.6 WTE)306 (261.8 WTE)186 (170.7 WTE)Source: Human Resources, Payroll, Travel & Subsistence (HRPTS) system# Excludes bank staff and staff on career break.Information on occupational therapists employed in HSC is published annually in the HSC Workforce Census, and can be found on the Department of Health website at the following link:https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/articles/staff-numbersOccupational therapists are not allocated to constituency areas.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail each time he has met with his UK counterparts in relation to the loss of EU Funding, for each of the last three years.
answer_text: When he was Finance Minister, Conor Murphy and departmental officials had considerable engagement with the British Government on the replacement of EU funding.  Since taking up post as Finance Minister in February 2024, I have continued to press on this important matter. In May 2024, I wrote to the then Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities, Michael Gove calling for urgent and substantive engagement on post-2025 funding. Following the General Election, I raised this in writing with the new Chief Secretary to the Treasury, Darren Jones MP, whom I subsequently met on 9th July. At this meeting, we discussed several issues including the need to work collaboratively going forward, and the new Government’s commitment to improving intergovernmental relationships.I have also written directly to the British Deputy Prime Minister, Angela Rayner MP, to press for clarity on post-2025 funding and the immediate implementation of the Labour Manifesto commitment to restore local decision-making in this regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the total number of approvals issued for (a) free school meals; and (b) uniform grants; and (ii) the average time taken from date of application submission to the granting of an approval, for this academic year.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that the total number of children and young people approved for free school meals and uniform grants as at Monday 12 February 2024 is as follows:(a)	Free school meals - 96,861 (b)	Uniform grants – 90,659 The average time taken to process an application for free school meals in the 2023/24 academic year is 16.56 days.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) what efforts her Department has made to identify pensioners impacted by the error in pension payments made by the Deparment for Work and Pensions; (ii) the estimated total amount underpaid; and (iii) to date, how many individuals in Northern Ireland have been compensated.
answer_text: Officials in my Department have worked closely with the Department for Work and Pensions to ensure that pensioners impacted in Northern Ireland are identified. There are 11,308 Northern Ireland residents identified as potentially having been underpaid State Pension.   Given the complex nature of this work it is difficult to estimate the total value of underpayments while the review of cases is ongoing.  To date, 8,653 cases have been investigated, with £10.5 million paid in arrears.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the number of pensioners in Northern Ireland who have been underpaid as a result of the error in pension payments made by the Department for Work and Pensions; and (ii) of these, how many are women.
answer_text: To date 2,104 pensioners in Northern Ireland have been identified as being underpaid State Pension. Of those identified 1,186 are women.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 3642/22-27, whether the upgrade works for Caw Roundabout will be a jointly funded endeavour between his Department and developers.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on street lighting provision for Springtown Industrial Estate in the Foyle constituency.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why temporary restrictions as part of the A6 Duncreggan to Derry dualling scheme, including lane closures and a 40mph speed limit, have not been lifted during periods when work is not underway in order to ease traffic flow in the area.
answer_text: Unfortunately my officials were unable to gather together the information sought by the member in the short amount of time I had remaining in office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) to detail the number of individuals subject to benefit (a) debt; and (b) overpayments; and (ii) the average time taken to pay off debt, for each of the last five years.
answer_text: (i) (a) The number of individuals currently subject to a benefit debt is 190,368. This figure includes Social Fund and Discretionary Support loan debts, benefit advances and benefit overpayments.(b) The number of individuals subject to a benefit overpayment is 87,505.This figure is included in (a) above.(ii) The average time taken for an individual to repay their social security debt is not captured as there are many variables which can influence this including, legislative requirements, the method of repayment and the number of debts an individual may have.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the roll out of the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer.
answer_text: I am pleased to announce that I have formally signed off on a programme of investment and agreed a set of key principles to frame the longer-term vision of The Northern Ireland Football Fund (previously known as the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer).My officials are working on detailed proposals, and I expect the first projects to receive letters of offer from my Department within this financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to provide an update on the business case required for the funding of the Northlands Addiction Centre in Foyle.
answer_text: I understand that this question relates to the commitment under Annex A to the “New Decade, New Approach” (NDNA) document, in the context of the agreement to restore the NI Executive, when the UK Government outlined Financial and Economic Commitments to Northern Ireland. Under the section Addressing Northern Ireland’s Unique Circumstances, the UK Government stated that it would make funding available for a range of projects aimed at supporting community and reconciliation initiatives to remove barriers, bring the people together and build a safer, more secure society, which could include areas such as additional funding to support the Derry/Londonderry addiction centre.Following discussions with the Northern Ireland Office (NIO), my officials, along with the Public Health Agency (PHA) and the Strategic Planning & Performance Group (SPPG) as the commissioners of substance use services, have been looking at how best to develop potential proposals for the allocation of funding under this commitment.As an initial step, a population needs-based assessment of substance use and related services in the Western Health & Social Care Trust area is now underway and a review of inpatient Tier 4 services across Northern Ireland is also being commissioned.In addition, my Department is leading on the implementation of Preventing Harm, Empowering Recovery, NI’s new substance use strategy. As part of this, SPPG in association with PHA are in the process of developing an approach to Multi-Agency Strategic Planning and Commissioning. It is the intention that a comprehensive, whole-system, planning approach to substance use will ensure that demographic trends of increasing demand and complexity, current and future resource, gaps, service pressures and workforce issues will be addressed.All of these pieces of work will be utilised so that my Department can make informed proposals to the NIO around this NDNA commitment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether she will implement a pay increase for Northern Ireland Housing Executive staff.
answer_text: I will, in line with DoF guidance, need to approve the Housing Executive’s annual pay award once an agreed settlement has been reached.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether consideration has been given to the introduction of a temporary eviction ban to protect tenants in private rented accommodation amid the cost of living crisis.
answer_text: I have no plans to bring forward legislation to introduce a temporary eviction ban. Court orders relating to evictions / repossessions may be actioned by the Enforcement of Judgments Office subject to the payment of the appropriate fee by the creditor. The Enforcements of Judgments Office is a division of the Northern Ireland Courts and Tribunals Service.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 3734/22-27, to detail the five schemes in Foyle which have been impacted by issues with one major contract.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“In October 2022 the Housing Executive’s Board took the decision to release 10 contractors from its CT055 planned maintenance contracts due to the significant inflationary increases in cost being experienced at the time. The Housing Executive have procured replacement contracts which commenced in September 2023 and are currently delivering works.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the total number of Disabled Facilities Grant applications where grant approval has been issued for 2021/22, broken down by council area.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“For the financial year 2024-25 the Housing Executive has so far issued 91 letters of approval for the mandatory Disabled Facilities Grants.  Below is a breakdown by Council area: Disabled Facilities Grants Approvals 2024-251 April to 13 May 2024Council AreaNumber issuedANTRIM AND NEWTOWNABBEY6ARDS AND NORTH DOWN4ARMAGH CITY BANBRIDGE AND CRAIGAVON11BELFAST13CAUSEWAY COAST AND GLENS8DERRY CITY AND STRABANE20FERMANAGH AND OMAGH10LISBURN AND CASTLEREAGH1MID AND EAST ANTRIM5MID ULSTER8NEWRY MOURNE AND DOWN5Total91”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what steps he has taken to recruit additional Occupational Therapists throughout all five trust areas to address growing demand for assessments.
answer_text: I announced on 11 May 2022 a £2.1m education and training package to boost the healthcare workforce which included an increase of 10 in the number of pre-registration Occupational Therapy training places, commissioned by my Department for 2022/23.Recruitment is a matter for individual HSC Trusts, as employers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether she has given consideration to introducing free school meal provision for all primary school children from Primary One to Primary Five, similar to the Scottish model.
answer_text: The Department is currently undertaking a review of Free School Meals and Uniform Grant eligibility criteria.  The review has considered a wide range of options that include the provision of a universal free school meals to some/all year groups as well as raising the income thresholds that apply to the eligibility criteria for applicants in receipt of Universal Credit or Working/Child Tax Credit. It is also actively considering the option for guidance on the costs of school uniforms to be on a statutory basis. My Department continues to work to develop a costed options paper and it is intended that a public consultation exercise will take place later in the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the recruitment of a perinatal mental health team in the Western Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: Good progress has been made in the recruitment of a perinatal mental health team in the Western Health and Social Care Trust.  A Nurse Co-ordinator has been appointed and a number of other posts have been offered and are due to commence next month. Interviews for both the midwife and health visitor posts are ongoing with a view to successful candidates taking up post early in the new year.

answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the NI Housing Executive. question_text: To ask the Minister of Health why patients here cannot access semaglutide and liraglutide weight loss medications on prescription.
answer_text: In December 2020, NICE published Technology Appraisal Guidance (TA664) recommending Liraglutide is prescribed in secondary care by a specialist multidisciplinary Tier 3 weight management service.  Northern Ireland does not currently have a Tier 3 weight management service.  At the beginning of 2019, my Department announced plans to explore options to develop a prototype Regional Obesity Management Service (ROMS), including both a Tier 3 weight management programme and a Tier 4 bariatric surgery service.  Unfortunately due to the Covid pandemic, development of the prototype had to be put on hold.  However, work is ongoing to ensure the foundations are put in place to allow for the future development of a ROMS, when resources allow.  In respect of semaglutide, on 17 June 2022, NICE published its final draft guidance recommending the use of semaglutide (Wegovy) as an option for managing overweight and obesity. A date for final guidance is yet to be determined. When the final guidance for this treatment is issued by NICE, my Department will consider it under the usual process for NICE Technology Appraisals as per the guidance set out in circular HSC (SQSD) 12/22 which is available at: https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/health/doh-hsc-sqsd-12-22.pdf

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) how many social security claimants in Northern Ireland are facing debt deductions from their payments; and (ii) how many of those claimants have requested lower debt deductions.
answer_text: (i) The number of social security claimants in Northern Ireland who are currently facing debt deductions from their benefit payments is 109,492. This figure includes individuals who are repaying Social Fund and Discretionary Support loan debts, benefit advances and benefit overpayments.(ii)The number of these claimants currently repaying their debt balance at a lower deduction rate is 26,206.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of claimants who have benefited from the Universal Credit Contingency Fund for the year 2021/22.
answer_text: In 2021/22, 7662 claimants received a Universal Credit Contingency Fund grant with an associated spend of £2,497,718.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the future educational provision at Ballougry Primary School in Foyle.
answer_text: Development Proposal (DP) 691 was published by the Education Authority (EA) on 27 September 2022.  It proposes that “Ballougry Primary School will discontinue with effect from 31 August 2023, or as soon as possible thereafter”.  This started a statutory two-month objection period which will end on 28 November 2022, during which time interested parties are able to submit their expressions of support or opposition to the Department of Education (Area Planning Policy Team,  Rathgael House, Balloo Road, Bangor, Co Down BT19 7PR) or e-mailed to dps@education-ni.gov.uk. At the end of the statutory objection period, Departmental officials will gather all the relevant information and evidence and will present these in a detailed submission to me for my consideration. Unfortunately, I am unable to offer a specific date for a decision.  I appreciate the importance of a timely decision regarding the proposals and I am acutely aware of the need for certainty for the interested parties.  I will not delay a decision any longer than necessary.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 3641/22-27.
answer_text: The Republic of Ireland (RoI) Reimbursement Scheme was put in place on a temporary basis for a period of 12 months from 1 July 2021 to help reduce Northern Ireland waiting lists and to provide an opportunity for some patients to have their treatment much earlier.  A further £5m was allocated to continue to operate the scheme beyond the planned 30 June 2022 deadline.  This further funding is exhausted and the scheme closed on 21 September 2022 to new applicants.The Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG), who administrate the temporary RoI cross border reimbursement scheme, do not record when patients have their treatment but rather when patients submit claims.  At 30 September the SPPG had processed 1605 patient claims and received a further 494 claims for payment.  It is therefore estimated that approximately 2099 patients had benefitted from the reimbursement scheme in the period July 2021 – Sept 2022. I am very conscious of pressing demands for extra funding across many areas of health and social care. As Minister, I have continued to do the best I can to deliver for the public with the resources I have available. Amid ongoing budget uncertainty, I am on record as warning that funding pressures in health may be significant by the second half of 2022/23. The financial situation will undoubtedly be constrained whatever the final budget settlement. However, because of these pressures, I regret that it has not been possible to assign funding to further extend the RoIRS.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether a pathway of monitoring and post-operative care will be developed for patients who access bariatric treatment abroad or outside of the NHS.
answer_text: As you will be aware, bariatric surgery is not currently commissioned in Northern Ireland. At the beginning of 2019, my Department announced plans to explore options to develop a prototype Regional Obesity Management Service (ROMS), including a Tier 4 bariatric surgery service, and a Task and Finish (T&F) Group was set up to take this forward.Currently, in the absence of a ROMS, where an individual has travelled abroad for bariatric surgery and has experienced complications on their return, they may access HSC services for emergency care if required. In addition to this, the ROMS T&F Group has also created a surgical single point of contact in each HSC Trust to assist GPs with any queries related to follow up care for patients who have undergone bariatric surgery abroad, and a separate contact to provide advice and support in relation to blood interpretation.The T&F Group continues to work towards ensuring the foundations are in place to allow for the future development of a ROMS, when resources allow.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to approve access to weight loss injection semaglutide for adults with at least one weight-related condition, as per NICE recommendations.
answer_text: My Department has a formal link with NICE under which NICE Technology Appraisals are reviewed locally for their legal and policy applicability here, and where found to be applicable, they are endorsed for implementation within Health and Social Care (HSC).When the final guidance for this treatment is issued by NICE, my Department will consider it under the usual process for NICE Technology Appraisals as per the guidance set out in circular HSC (SQSD) 12/22 which is available at: https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/sites/default/files/publications/health/doh-hsc-sqsd-12-22.pdf

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether consideration has been given to introducing a tier three weight management programme for patients struggling with severe and complex obesity issues.
answer_text: At the beginning of 2019, my Department announced plans to explore options to develop a prototype Regional Obesity Management Service (ROMS), including both a Tier 3 weight management programme and a Tier 4 bariatric surgery service.  A Task & Finish (T&F) Group was set up to take this forward.  However, as a result of the pandemic, the Department had to redirect significant resources to address all aspects of the immediate and critical issues associated with Covid-19, meaning that this work unfortunately had to be put on hold.  As we now emerge from the pandemic, our immediate focus is on rebuilding the Health and Social Care (HSC) service. However, work is underway through the T&F Group to ensure the foundations are put in place to allow for the future development of a ROMS, when resources allow.  In the meantime, arrangements have been put in place to ensure assessment can be provided to those people presenting to HSC facilities with complications associated with obesity. My Department is also currently working on the development of a new Obesity Strategy for Northern Ireland which is due to be published in 2023. The Strategy will outline policy on both the prevention and treatment of obesity, including surgical intervention.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, given the current amber alert over NHS blood supplies, what plans there are to facilitate blood donation drives, particularly within the Foyle constituency.
answer_text: Following media reports that NHS Blood and Transplant had declared an amber alert, the Northern Ireland Blood Transfusion Service (NIBTS) found that there was an increase in the number of people willing to donate.  In response NIBTS implemented a short-term interim online form for interested donors to complete. NIBTS have taken several actions to increase donations throughout the year including:appointment of a new blood donor engagement manager;a new radio campaign commencing in the next week, alongside promoted paid media to re-engage previous donors and also to recruit new donors; implementation of overtime to ensure that the recent increase in applications of willing donors are processed quickly and efficiently; andNIBTS are also in the process of procuring and implementing an online booking system which will improve donor experience in the long term.In relation to the Foyle area NIBTS have:attended a number of sessions this year in the Foyle area.  They been in the area 4 times within the past month and have a session scheduled in Claudy next week;paid targeted media within the area, utilising contacts in the Western Trust to promote messaging; andoutreached to local businesses within the area to see if they will promote the message through their internal or external profiles.NIBTS have also collected almost 1600 donations in the Foyle area since January this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of patients on the waiting list for IVF treatment, broken down by how long they have been waiting.
To ask the Minister for Communities what steps her Department has taken to implement an updated Empty Homes Strategy.
answer_text: My Department has developed a draft Housing Supply Strategy which aims to address the wider challenges facing housing supply. This includes consideration of how we can make best use of existing assets and also ensuring we protect existing homes to retain them in the supply system for longer. 		I am currently reviewing the Strategy with the aim of bringing a finalised version to the Executive for its consideration and endorsement in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for (i) his assessment of the current capacity at the Regional Fertility Centre to facilitate the numbers of patients currently awaiting treatment; and (ii) has consideration been given to outsourcing treatment to private clinics.
answer_text: Services at the Regional Fertility Centre (RFC) are now back to pre-Covid activity level through the relaxation of social distancing and the support of my Department with non-recurrent waiting list initiative funding.  The RFC has the capacity to facilitate patients currently awaiting treatment in a timely manner.It is therefore not deemed necessary to consider outsourcing treatment to the independent sector at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the A6 Derry to Dungiven dualling scheme will be completed.
answer_text: My officials are working closely with the contractor to have this strategically important project completed as quickly as possible. Current market volatility and supply chain restrictions continue to impact on project delivery but it expected that the road will open within the next few months.
